title: limonium vulgare subsp. serotinum
intro: Limonium vulgare subsp. serotinum, also known as sea lavender, is a perennial plant species that belongs to the family Plumbaginaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include antiseptic, antispasmodic, and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of sea lavender include reducing anxiety, improving sleep quality, and alleviating symptoms of menopause, as well as its ability to soothe digestive issues and reduce inflammation. Sea lavender can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or essential oil, which can be used to treat various health conditions, making it a valuable addition to natural remedies.
